interfaces.name: PlanKind
interfaces.signature: enum PlanKind: String, Codable { case active, template }
interfaces.notes: Templates have no serveTime until duplicated into an active plan; free tier never creates templates. Lives in Domain/Models/.

interfaces.name: PlanStatus
interfaces.signature: enum PlanStatus: String, Codable { case draft, started, completed }
interfaces.notes: draft = pre-Start (relative, editable); started = schedule committed, edits locked; completed = all steps done.

interfaces.name: StepKind
interfaces.signature: enum StepKind: String, Codable { case active, passive }
interfaces.notes: Drives flame-vs-clock glyph; never color, per accessibility rule.

interfaces.name: StepRuntimeStatus
interfaces.signature: enum StepRuntimeStatus: String, Codable { case upcoming, inProgress, doneEarly, completed }
interfaces.notes: Computed by SchedulingEngine.deriveLiveState, never stored. Lives in Scheduling/, not Domain/.

interfaces.name: Plan
interfaces.signature: @Model final class Plan { var id: UUID; var kind: PlanKind; var serveTime: Date?; var status: PlanStatus; var createdAt: Date; var startedAt: Date?; var completedAt: Date?; var templateName: String?; var lateShiftTotalSeconds: TimeInterval; @Relationship(deleteRule: .cascade, inverse: \Dish.plan) var dishes: [Dish] }
interfaces.notes: Reference type despite 'struct' kind tag (SwiftData @Model requires a class). Lives in Domain/Models/, imports SwiftData -- kept physically separate from Scheduling/ which must not.

interfaces.name: Dish
interfaces.signature: @Model final class Dish { var id: UUID; var name: String; var entryOrder: Int; var colorIndex: Int; var plan: Plan?; @Relationship(deleteRule: .cascade, inverse: \Step.dish) var steps: [Step] }
interfaces.notes: entryOrder is the tie-break sort key for simultaneous step start times; colorIndex maps into the fixed 6-8 hue dish palette, assigned at creation.

interfaces.name: Step
interfaces.signature: @Model final class Step { var id: UUID; var label: String; var plannedDuration: TimeInterval; var kind: StepKind; var stepOrder: Int; var scheduledStartAt: Date?; var scheduledEndAt: Date?; var completedEarlyAt: Date?; var dish: Dish? }
interfaces.notes: plannedDuration + stepOrder are entry data used pre-Start. scheduledStartAt/scheduledEndAt are entry data too (written once at Start, mutated only by applyRunningLate or a forgotten-dish merge) -- NOT a cache of a live computation.

interfaces.name: StepInput
interfaces.signature: struct StepInput { let stepID: UUID; let dishID: UUID; let dishName: String; let colorIndex: Int; let dishEntryOrder: Int; let label: String; let duration: TimeInterval; let kind: StepKind; let stepOrder: Int }
interfaces.notes: Framework-free value-type snapshot fed into SchedulingEngine; built from Plan/Dish/Step by AppModel, never a raw @Model reference. Lives in Scheduling/.

interfaces.name: TimelineItem
interfaces.signature: struct TimelineItem: Identifiable { let id: UUID; let dishID: UUID; let dishName: String; let colorIndex: Int; let label: String; let kind: StepKind; let startAt: Date; let endAt: Date; let isPastDue: Bool; let runtimeStatus: StepRuntimeStatus }
interfaces.notes: Pure output value type; safe to pass directly into SwiftUI views and into ActivityAttributes.ContentState. Lives in Scheduling/, tested via SchedulingEngineTests fixtures.

interfaces.name: MergedTimeline
interfaces.signature: struct MergedTimeline { let serveTime: Date; let items: [TimelineItem]; let generatedAt: Date }
interfaces.notes: Used for both the Plan Builder live preview (draft, unpersisted) and as the basis for FullTimeline in Start Confirmation.

interfaces.name: LiveCountdownState
interfaces.signature: struct LiveCountdownState { let dominant: TimelineItem?; let secondaryInProgress: [TimelineItem]; let allUpcoming: [TimelineItem]; let nextRecomputeAt: Date? }
interfaces.notes: nextRecomputeAt is the soonest upcoming startAt/endAt boundary; drives the discrete recompute timer so per-second numeral ticks never trigger a full re-sort.

interfaces.name: SchedulingEngine
interfaces.signature: enum SchedulingEngine { static func previewTimeline(serveTime: Date, dishes: [Dish]) -> MergedTimeline; static func commitSchedule(serveTime: Date, dishes: [Dish], now: Date) -> [UUID: (start: Date, end: Date)]; static func deriveLiveState(items: [TimelineItem], now: Date) -> LiveCountdownState; static func applyRunningLate(items: [TimelineItem], shiftBy: TimeInterval, now: Date) -> [TimelineItem]; static func markStepDoneEarly(items: [TimelineItem], stepID: UUID, now: Date) -> [TimelineItem] }
interfaces.notes: Lives at BackTimer/Scheduling/SchedulingEngine.swift, Foundation-only, no SwiftData/ActivityKit/UserNotifications imports -- physically separated from Domain/ so this constraint is visually enforced, not just policy. 100% covered by BackTimerTests/SchedulingEngineTests.swift: tie-break, DST spring-forward/fall-back, midnight-crossing, past-due-at-creation, running-late cascade incl. in-flight passive step.

interfaces.name: PersistenceController
interfaces.signature: struct PersistenceController { static let shared: PersistenceController; let container: ModelContainer; init(inMemory: Bool = false) }
interfaces.notes: Lives at BackTimer/Domain/Persistence/PersistenceController.swift. Single local SwiftData store, no App Group. inMemory init supports BackTimerTests/AppModelTests and SwiftUI previews without touching disk.

interfaces.name: FreeTierLimits
interfaces.signature: struct FreeTierLimits { static let maxDishesPerPlan = 3; static let maxConcurrentActivePlans = 1; static let templatesAllowed = false }
interfaces.notes: Lives in BackTimer/Services/. Checked by EntitlementStore.canAddDish/canCreateConcurrentPlan/canUseTemplates, gated from cached entitlement so it works offline.

interfaces.name: SubscriptionTier
interfaces.signature: enum SubscriptionTier: String, Codable { case free, subscribed }
interfaces.notes: Cached at launch/foreground via Transaction.currentEntitlements when connectivity exists; never a hard network-blocking check.

interfaces.name: EntitlementError
interfaces.signature: enum EntitlementError: Error { case purchaseFailed(String); case restoreFoundNothing; case storeKitUnavailable }
interfaces.notes: restoreFoundNothing is worded as an expected empty result in the UI, not a failure, per the locked Settings/Paywall state model.

interfaces.name: EntitlementProviding
interfaces.signature: protocol EntitlementProviding: AnyObject { var tier: SubscriptionTier { get }; var isRestoring: Bool { get }; var lastError: EntitlementError? { get }; func refresh() async; func purchase(_ product: Product) async throws; func restorePurchases() async; func canAddDish(currentCount: Int) -> Bool; func canCreateConcurrentPlan(currentActiveCount: Int) -> Bool; func canUseTemplates() -> Bool }
interfaces.notes: Concrete impl EntitlementStore (@Observable @MainActor) wraps StoreKit 2. Protocol exists so BackTimerTests/AppModelTests can inject a fake; BackTimerUITests/PaywallUITests exercises the real impl via .storekit config + SKTestSession.

interfaces.name: NotificationSchedulingError
interfaces.signature: enum NotificationSchedulingError: Error { case authorizationDenied; case systemSchedulingFailed(String); case pendingLimitExceeded }
interfaces.notes: Surfaces into Start Confirmation's error state, which rolls the plan back to .draft rather than starting with missing alerts -- covered by AppModelTests.testStartPlan_rollsBackOnSchedulingFailure.

interfaces.name: NotificationScheduling
interfaces.signature: protocol NotificationScheduling { func requestAuthorization() async -> Bool; func scheduleWindow(planID: UUID, items: [TimelineItem], now: Date, windowSize: Int) async throws; func cancelAll(planID: UUID) async; func pendingRequestIDs(planID: UUID) async -> Set<String>; func reconcile(expected: [TimelineItem], planID: UUID) async -> Bool }
interfaces.notes: Concrete impl NotificationCoordinator wraps UNUserNotificationCenter, lives in BackTimer/Services/. reconcile diffs actual-pending vs expected -- the concrete signal for the 'notification silently failed' error state. Reschedule triggers: Start, every scenePhase->.active, every notification delivery/response, every applyRunningLate call.

interfaces.name: LiveActivityError
interfaces.signature: enum LiveActivityError: Error { case authorizationDenied; case startFailed(String) }
interfaces.notes: Distinct from NotificationSchedulingError since the two permissions are denied/granted independently per the locked degraded-mode design.

interfaces.name: LiveActivityManaging
interfaces.signature: protocol LiveActivityManaging { var isActivityActive: Bool { get }; func requestAuthorization() -> Bool; func start(planID: UUID, initialState: LiveCountdownState) throws; func update(_ state: LiveCountdownState) async; func end(finalState: LiveCountdownState?) async; func detectForceEnded() -> Bool }
interfaces.notes: Concrete impl LiveActivityCoordinator wraps ActivityKit, lives in BackTimer/Services/. detectForceEnded checked on foreground to trigger the force-ended-recovery banner.

interfaces.name: BackTimerActivityAttributes
interfaces.signature: struct BackTimerActivityAttributes: ActivityAttributes { public struct ContentState: Codable, Hashable { var dominantLabel: String; var dominantColorIndex: Int; var dominantEndAt: Date; var secondaryCount: Int } var planID: UUID }
interfaces.notes: Lives at BackTimerWidgetExtension/BackTimerActivityAttributes.swift with dual target membership (app + extension) -- the ONLY file shared between targets, no App Group. BackTimerLiveActivity.swift in the extension target renders all five presentation contexts (lock screen, DI compact-leading/trailing, minimal, expanded) purely from ContentState.

interfaces.name: AppRoute
interfaces.signature: enum AppRoute: Equatable { case liveCountdown(planID: UUID); case home }
interfaces.notes: Consumed once on launch/foreground by AppModel.bootstrap; cleared after navigation so it never re-fires.

interfaces.name: DeepLinkRouter
interfaces.signature: @Observable @MainActor final class DeepLinkRouter { var pendingRoute: AppRoute?; func handleNotificationResponse(_ response: UNNotificationResponse); func consumePendingRoute() -> AppRoute? }
interfaces.notes: Tapping a step-transition notification must deep-link straight into Live Countdown, not just foreground the app.

interfaces.name: AppModel
interfaces.signature: @Observable @MainActor final class AppModel { var navigationPath: NavigationPath; var activePlan: Plan?; var liveCountdownState: LiveCountdownState?; var pendingBanner: PermissionBannerKind?; func bootstrap() async; func startPlan(_ plan: Plan) async throws; func applyRunningLate(shiftBy: TimeInterval); func markStepDoneEarly(_ step: Step) }
interfaces.notes: Lives at BackTimer/App/AppModel.swift. Single root owner of ModelContext and navigation. bootstrap() implements cold-launch shortcut: checks for an active started plan before Home ever renders, then checks DeepLinkRouter.pendingRoute. Orchestration logic covered by BackTimerTests/AppModelTests.swift against fake NotificationScheduling/LiveActivityManaging/EntitlementProviding; end-to-end happy path covered once by BackTimerUITests/CoreFlowUITests.swift.

interfaces.name: ErrorBannerModel
interfaces.signature: struct ErrorBannerModel: Identifiable { let id: UUID; let message: String; let severity: BannerSeverity }
interfaces.notes: Lives in BackTimer/Shared/. Backs the global no-modal-alerts rule -- every error in this app renders inline/banner, never as a system alert.

interfaces.name: BannerSeverity
interfaces.signature: enum BannerSeverity: String { case info, warning, error }
interfaces.notes: warning used for permission-degraded states, error for actionable failures like a failed purchase or failed scheduling commit.

interfaces.name: PermissionBannerKind
interfaces.signature: enum PermissionBannerKind: Equatable { case notificationsDenied; case liveActivityDenied; case forceEndedRecovery }
interfaces.notes: Three visually and textually distinct banners per the locked design -- never merged into one generic 'something's wrong' banner.
